subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Drain Entrapment
description: A micro-lesson that makes Drain Entrapment usable.

hook.text: A child sits on a pool drain. The suction is so powerful it holds them underwater.

buildup.text: Pool drains create suction forces strong enough to trap hair, limbs, and even torsos.

discovery.text: The Virginia Graeme Baker Act now requires anti-entrapment drain covers in all public pools.

twist.text: The law was named after a 7-year-old who was held underwater by a spa drain and drowned.

climax.text: Knowing where drains are and keeping children away from them is essential pool safety.

punchline.text: A flat drain cover costs $20. A child's life is priceless. Check every pool.

quiz.question: What does the Virginia Graeme Baker Act require?
options: Anti-entrapment drain covers in public pools
